title: Naval Platform MRO Base
entry_mechanism: Establish dedicated naval maintenance facility at Jubail with Navantia technical partnership
executive_summary:
Saudi Arabia's Royal Saudi Naval Forces (RSNF) operate a fleet of frigates, corvettes, mine countermeasure vessels, and patrol craft that require periodic dry dock maintenance, hull inspections, machinery overhaul, and systems updates. Today, major maintenance is performed at foreign shipyards — primarily in Spain, France, and the United States — at significant cost and with extended periods of operational unavailability. The Avante 2200 corvette programme creates an immediate requirement for in-Kingdom naval maintenance: Navantia's programme commitments include support for a Saudi naval maintenance capability.

The global naval MRO market was valued at $26 billion in 2024. Saudi Arabia's naval fleet generates an estimated $400–600 million per year in maintenance expenditure, of which less than 20% is performed in-Kingdom. A dry dock and maintenance facility at Jubail — adjacent to the King Abdulaziz Naval Base — would serve the Avante 2200 programme immediately and create the infrastructure for long-term Saudi naval industrial sovereignty.

The strategic importance of in-Kingdom naval MRO extends beyond cost: a Saudi naval fleet dependent on foreign shipyards for maintenance is vulnerable to political disruption in precisely the moments of heightened tension when operational readiness is most critical.

companies.name: Navantia
companies.rationale: Navantia built the Avante 2200 and holds the technical documentation. A Navantia-supported Saudi naval MRO facility is the logical extension of the corvette programme.

companies.name: Naval Group (France)
companies.rationale: Naval Group operates naval shipyards and MRO facilities across multiple countries. Non-ITAR French technology and active Gulf engagement.
companies.contact: Naval Group VP International Naval Support

companies.name: BAE Systems Maritime
companies.rationale: BAE Systems operates naval dockyards and has deep experience in warship maintenance. Active Saudi relationship through other defence programmes.

companies.name: Fincantieri
companies.rationale: Fincantieri designs and operates naval dry dock facilities and could advise on the Jubail facility design and operational model.

companies.name: Damen Shipyards
companies.rationale: Damen supplies patrol craft to the Gulf region and has an established regional repair network that could anchor a Saudi facility.
companies.contact: Damen Naval VP After Sales, Middle East

partners.name: Royal Commission for Jubail / Jubail Industrial City
partners.current: Industrial port infrastructure; heavy industry support services; existing marine berths
partners.upgrade: Develop dedicated naval dry dock within Jubail industrial port; naval-grade berths and crane infrastructure; security clearance zone for warship maintenance
partners.timeline: 36 months to first dry dock operational
partners.anchor: Royal Saudi Navy + SAMI + Royal Commission

partners.name: Saudi Aramco Offshore Engineering
partners.current: Offshore platform maintenance; marine vessel support; diving and ROV services
partners.upgrade: Extend offshore engineering capability to naval vessel hull inspection and maintenance; transfer underwater hull survey capability to RSNF vessel maintenance
partners.timeline: 18 months to first naval hull inspection contract

partners.name: SAMI Naval Division
partners.current: Naval programme management; Avante 2200 programme oversight
partners.upgrade: Establish SAMI-operated naval MRO centre at Jubail; Navantia technical licence for Avante 2200 maintenance; hire 100 Saudi naval maintenance technicians

partners.name: Saudi Ports Authority / Mawani
partners.current: Commercial port operations; vessel maintenance support services
partners.upgrade: Establish naval maintenance zone within Jubail commercial port; dual-use dry dock serving commercial and naval vessels; Lloyd's Register certified facilities
